items.title: Team USA Expected Olympic Dominance. Not Anymore | WSJ
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>The American Olympic team is suddenly on track for its fewest winter golds since 1998 — one week into the 2026 Games. WSJ's Joshua Robinson reports from Milan. 

#WSJ #olympics #teamUSA</article>

items.title: I Curled Against a Professional. How Hard Can It Be?
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>As curling events continue to take place at the 2026 Winter Olympics in Italy, WSJ's Laine Higgins curled against former U.S. curling champion Bill Stopera to find out just how hard it is. 

#Olympics #Curling #WSJ</article>

items.title: Why Caterpillar’s Stock Is Benefiting From the AI Boom
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>Shares of Caterpillar, the maker of mining and construction machinery, are up more than 30% since year-end, making it the biggest contributor to the Dow Jones Industrial Average. WSJ’s Jonathan Weil explains what’s behind the moves. 

#Caterpillar #AI #WSJ</article>

items.title: This Elite Coast Guard Unit Showed Us How They Train for Key Missions
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>With its special forces playing a key role in Trump’s national security missions, the Coast Guard is standing up a new command for its elite teams. These special forces units have ramped up drug interdictions in the Caribbean and the Pacific, leading seizers of oil tankers.

WSJ’s Shelby Holliday visited a unit in Miami to learn more. 

#CoastGuard #Trump #WSJ</article>

items.title: Is Kim Jong Un's Young Daughter the Chosen One?
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>South Korea’s spy agency believes Kim Jong Un may be positioning his daughter to be his successor. But who is she? WSJ’s Korea Bureau Chief Tim Martin explains.

#NorthKorea #KimJongUn #WSJ</article>

items.title: How NATO Hunts for Russian Submarines in the Arctic
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>WSJ’s Daniel Michaels joins a Spanish warship for NATO military exercises as the alliance tries to show President Trump it can take on Russia’s growing presence in the region.

#NATO #Russia #WSJ</article>

items.title: This Swedish Hotel Is Made of 30K Tons of Ice and Snow
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>Nearly 100 workers race to Jukkasjarvi, Sweden, each November to build the world-renowned Icehotel. They have only weeks to create the entire structure and all the rooms before mid-December, and the materials pose some unique logistical challenges. The hotel includes a main hall, a ceremony hall for weddings and 12 suites custom-made by artists from around the world.

WSJ traveled to Sweden to see the steps and the challenges of how this hotel is made every year from 30,000 tons of ice and snow.

#Hotels #Travel #WSJ</article>

items.title: FBI Releases Video of Masked Person in Nancy Guthrie Investigation
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>The FBI shared footage of an armed individual appearing to tamper with Nancy Guthrie's camera on the morning of her disappearance.⁠
⁠
Guthrie, 84 years old, is the mother of “Today” co-anchor Savannah Guthrie, and her sudden disappearance has made national headlines. Nancy Guthrie was first reported missing on Feb. 1 and authorities are investigating her disappearance as a crime.⁠

#FBI #Guthrie #WSJ</article>

items.title: Why Hotels Are Ditching Bathroom Doors
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>First they came for the closets. Then they took the bath tubs. Now, hotels are stripping away the only thing separating us from the animals: the bathroom door.⁠

#Hotels #Design #WSJ</article>

items.title: Olympic Figure Skating: Why Ilia Malinin's Backflip Isn't Worth Any Points
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>Ilia Malinin cemented himself as a greatest-of-all-time when he claimed figure skating’s holy grail–a quadruple axel. But it’s his backflip that could make him immortal.⁠

#FigureSkating #Olympics #WSJ</article>

items.title: I Tried Curling to Find Out What Sweepers Actually Do
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>The United States will face off against Sweden on Tuesday in the gold medal match for curling mixed doubles at the Olympic Winter Games. For viewers tuning in, one part of the sport often raises the same question. WSJ's Laine Higgins tried curling to find out once and for all: Why exactly do they sweep?⁠

#Curling #Olympics #WSJ</article>
